content: Diagnosis requires tissue confirmation. PSA elevation triggers evaluation: consider age-specific ranges, PSA velocity, free/total PSA ratio, PHI (Prostate Health Index), 4Kscore. Multiparametric MRI (mpMRI) increasingly used before biopsy; PI-RADS score 1-5 predicts clinically significant cancer; MRI-targeted biopsy superior to systematic alone (PRECISION trial). Transrectal or transperineal biopsy: systematic 12-core standard; MRI-targeted cores added for suspicious lesions. Pathology: Gleason score from biopsy/surgical specimen; percent core involvement; perineural invasion. Staging: bone scan and CT/MRI for high-risk disease or symptoms; PSMA PET/CT increasingly used (more sensitive than conventional imaging, detects recurrence at lower PSA). Post-prostatectomy monitoring: PSA should be undetectable (<0.1); rising PSA indicates recurrence. Biochemical recurrence definition: post-radiation PSA nadir + 2 ng/mL (Phoenix criteria); post-prostatectomy PSA ≥0.2 ng/mL confirmed.
